narrative.headline: 2020 Toyota Sienna: power train leads a small set of owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 35 complaints with NHTSA, a relatively low count. The power train is the most reported area at 23%, followed by a three-way tie among the engine, fuel/propulsion system, and electrical system at 17% each. No crashes, injuries, or deaths are reported. Two reports mention a fire (one engine, one fuel/propulsion system), so those areas are worth a closer look.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 8 reports (23%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. One owner describes a loose hose in the axle leaking fluid.

issues.sample: WHILE DRIVING ON HWY, SUDDENLY ENGINE LIGHT CAME ON. ALSO I LOST ACCELERATION. WHEN I PRESS GAS PEDAL NO RESPONSE FROM CAR. VEHICLE WAS  SLOWING DOWN SO PULLED INTO GAS STATION. WHEN I STOPPED THE CAR, ENGINE LIGHT WAS STILL ON, TRACTION LIGHT CAME ON AND STEERING SYSTEM  LIGHT C
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 6 reports (17%), including 1 fire, with no crashes or injuries reported. One owner describes the engine light coming on and losing acceleration while driving.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 6 reports (17%), including 1 fire, with no crashes or injuries reported. One owner mentions a fuel system recall where the repair part was unavailable.

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: 3 reports (9%), the fewest here, with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. One owner describes a rear door unlocking and opening on its own.
